nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### This n8n template allows you to use AI to generate logos or images which mimic visual styles of other logos or images. The model used to generate the images is Google's Imagen 3.0.

With this template, users will be able to automate design and marketing tasks such as creating variants of existing designs, remixing existing assets to validate different styles and explore a range of designs which would have been otherwise too expensive and time-consming previously.

### How it works
* A form trigger is used to capture the source image to reference styles from and a prompt for the target image to generate.
* The source image is passed to Gemini 2.0 to be analysed and its visual style and tone extracted as a detailed description.
* This visual style description is then combined with the user's initial target image prompt. This final prompt is given to Imagen 3.0 to generate the images.
* A quick webpage is put together with the generated images to present back to the user.
* If the user provided an email address, a copy of this HTML page will be sent.

### How to use
* Ensure the workflow is live to share the form publicly.
* The source image must be accessible to your n8n instance - either a public image of the internet or within your network.

### Requirements
* Gemini for LLM and Imagen model.
* Cloudinary for image CDN.
* Gmail for email sending.

### Customising this workflow
* Feel free to swap any of these out for tools and services you prefer.
* Want to fully automate? Switch the form trigger for a webhook trigger!


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

assignments.name: numberSamples

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Palm  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On form submission".
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Imagen 3.0" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Form Validation" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Retry Form" node (n8n-nodes-base.form) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Upload to Cloudinary" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert to File1" node (n8n-nodes-base.convertToFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate HTML" node (n8n-nodes-base.html) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert to File" node (n8n-nodes-base.convertToFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Form Ending" node (n8n-nodes-base.form) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Has Email?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Results to Email" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Image to Base64" node (n8n-nodes-base.extractFromFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gemini 2.0" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Variables".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
